name: Guatemala

colors.symbolism: the Pacific Ocean and the Caribbean Sea between which the country lies; also justice and loyalty
the laurel and olive branches and the resplendent quetzal's plumage
the gold of the scroll and detailing on the coat of arms
the rifles and sword on the coat of arms

description: Three equal vertical stripes of light blue, white, and light blue (hoist to fly), with the national coat of arms centred on the white stripe.
history: Adopted on 17 August 1871 after the Liberal Revolution under President Miguel Garcia Granados. The blue-white-blue pattern derives from the flag of the Federal Republic of Central America (1823-1841). The coat of arms was designed by Swiss artist Johann-Baptist Frener.

features.symbolism: the blue stripes represent Guatemala's geographic position between the Pacific and Atlantic oceans; the white stripe represents purity and integrity.

a resplendent quetzal (the national bird, symbol of liberty) atop a scroll inscribed 'LIBERTAD 15 DE SEPTIEMBRE DE 1821' (date of independence), over two crossed Remington rifles and two crossed swords, all wreathed in laurel branches representing victory.
